profile.authenticate.description: Exchanges a profile's credentials for an authorization token. The returned `authorizationToken` is an opaque string — treat it as a bearer secret and pass it on every subsequent API call as the `X-DPT-AUTHORIZATION` header. See the [Getting started](/guides/getting-started.html) guide for the end-to-end flow and the [Protocol overview](/guides/protocol-overview.html) for the encryption layers.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The username the profile was registered with.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: The profile's password.

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this token expires.

responses.description: Credentials rejected, or the profile's public key has not yet been registered.

profile.register-profile.description: Creates a new profile with the supplied username and password. This endpoint is asynchronous: it returns a `commandId` immediately and the real outcome lands on the WebSocket proxy as an `X-DPT-CAB-ID` message. The profile is **not yet usable** until you also register a public key against it. See [Getting started](/guides/getting-started.html) for the full provisioning sequence.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Desired username. Must be unique across the instance.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password for the new profile. Entropy requirements apply.

responses.description: Registration accepted; await the `X-DPT-CAB-ID` WebSocket message for final outcome.

commandId.description: Correlator for the async ack that will arrive on the WebSocket.

public-key.register-public-key.description: Registers the public key that activates a profile. Until this call succeeds, the profile is inert: authentication will reject its credentials and no write operation will succeed. The `publicKey` field is a signed Curve25519 key encoded as 200 hex characters: `[4-byte version tag][32-byte pubkey][64-byte integrity hash]`. This endpoint is asynchronous; await the `X-DPT-CAB-ID` message for final outcome.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: The profile to attach this key to.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: The profile's password, used to authorise the attachment.

parameters.name: publicKey
parameters.description: Signed Curve25519 public key, 200 hex characters: `[4-byte version tag][32-byte pubkey][64-byte integrity hash]`.

responses.description: Key registration accepted; await the `X-DPT-CAB-ID` WebSocket message for final outcome.

data-token.reserve-token-space.description: Opens a reservation that a subsequent [`exchange-data-for-token`](#tag/data-token/operation/data-token.exchange-data-for-token) call can fill. The reservation carries your chosen `context` (free-form string — commonly used like a directory name) and a `ttl` in seconds after which an unused reservation expires. Asynchronous: poll the returned `commandId`, or await the matching `X-DPT-CAB-ID` message on the WebSocket. See the [Store and retrieve](/guides/store-and-retrieve.html) walkthrough for the end-to-end flow.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Free-form label attached to the reservation. Commonly used to group related tokens (e.g. a directory-style path).

parameters.name: ttl
parameters.description: Time-to-live, in seconds, after which an unfilled reservation expires.

responses.description: Reservation accepted; await the `X-DPT-CAB-ID` WebSocket message for final outcome.

data-token.retrieve-temporary-data-token.description: Once a reservation has settled (the `X-DPT-CAB-ID` ack has arrived with `command_status: 2`), call this endpoint with the reservation's `commandId` to receive the temporary token you exchange data against, plus a pre-authenticated URL for the upstream file-storage service used by the streaming upload path.

parameters.name: commandId
parameters.description: The command id returned by [`reserve-token-space`](#tag/data-token/operation/data-token.reserve-token-space).

responses.description: The reservation was found and settled.

token.description: Temporary token to pass as `temporaryTokenSpace` when exchanging data.

stream.description: Pre-authenticated URL on the upstream FSD for direct binary upload. Use for payloads larger than ~50 KB.

responses.description: The reservation does not exist, has not yet settled, or has expired.

data-token.exchange-data-for-token.description: Uploads an AES-256-GCM-encrypted payload against a temporary token reservation. The `data` field is base64 of the wire format `[12-byte IV][ciphertext][16-byte auth tag]` — see [Protocol overview → Encryption](/guides/protocol-overview.html#the-encryption-model). Use this endpoint for payloads up to ~50 KB; for larger payloads, POST the raw ciphertext to the `stream` URL returned by [`retrieve-temporary-data-token`](#tag/data-token/operation/data-token.retrieve-temporary-data-token) instead.

parameters.name: temporaryTokenSpace
parameters.description: The temporary token from [`retrieve-temporary-data-token`](#tag/data-token/operation/data-token.retrieve-temporary-data-token).

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Base64 of the AES-256-GCM wire format: `[12-byte IV][ciphertext][16-byte auth tag]`.

responses.description: Payload accepted; await the `X-DPT-CAB-ID` WebSocket message for final outcome.

data-token.configure-file-meta.description: Commits file metadata against a filled temporary-token reservation and returns the **permanent data token** — the opaque string you persist in your own system as the long-lived handle for this payload. Every subsequent retrieve and lifecycle call uses this permanent token.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The temporary token from [`retrieve-temporary-data-token`](#tag/data-token/operation/data-token.retrieve-temporary-data-token).

parameters.name: fileName
parameters.description: Original filename, for display and retrieval hints.

parameters.name: extension
parameters.description: File extension without leading dot.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Matches the `context` used when the token was reserved.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Plaintext payload size in bytes.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Virtual path within the context namespace.

token.description: Permanent data token. Persist this — it is your long-lived handle.

data-token.request-data.description: Initiates a retrieval for a previously-stored token. The HTTP response headers include `x-dpt-cab-id` and `x-dpt-cab-request-id`; the actual payload (or a downstream stream authorisation) arrives as an `X-DPT-CAB-REQUEST-ID` message on the WebSocket. Branch on the message's `output_type`: `0` means the `body` is your AES ciphertext base64-encoded inline; `1` means the `body` is auth for a streamed download from the downstream file-storage service (await a `STREAM-READY` message, then `GET https://downstream…/?auth=<body>`). See [Store and retrieve](/guides/store-and-retrieve.html#step-5-retrieve-it-back) for the full branch.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: The permanent data token from [`configure-file-meta`](#tag/data-token/operation/data-token.configure-file-meta).

responses.description: Request accepted. Read `x-dpt-cab-request-id` from the response headers and await the matching `X-DPT-CAB-REQUEST-ID` message on the WebSocket.
The token does not exist or has been deleted.

public-key.retrieve-public-key.description: Returns the signed Curve25519 public key you registered with [`register-public-key`](#tag/public-key/operation/public-key.register-public-key), in the 200-hex-character signed format.

public-key.retrieve-cab-public-key.description: Returns the engine's own signed public key, used when you need to encrypt content *to* the engine or verify server-signed responses. Same 200-hex-character format as profile keys.

oauth.register-oauth-application.description: Registers a third-party OAuth application under the caller's profile. Returns an `applicationId` that can be fed to [`retrieve-oauth-app-configuration`](#tag/oauth/operation/oauth.retrieve-oauth-app-configuration) for the application's client credentials. See the [OAuth applications](/guides/oauth-applications.html) guide for the end-to-end flow.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Display name for the application.

parameters.name: redirectUri
parameters.description: OAuth redirect URI to which authorization codes are sent.

parameters.name: scopes
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of scopes the application may request.

responses.description: Registration accepted; await the WebSocket ack.

oauth.retrieve-oauth-app-configuration.description: Returns the `clientId`, `clientSecret`, configured `redirectUri`, and granted `scopes` for a registered OAuth application. The `clientSecret` is sensitive — treat it like an API key.

parameters.name: applicationId
parameters.description: Id returned by [`register-oauth-application`](#tag/oauth/operation/oauth.register-oauth-application).

oauth.request-oauth-app-authorization-co.description: The user-consent leg of the OAuth authorization-code flow. In interactive integrations, this is typically surfaced via a browser redirect rather than a direct API call — but the wire call is documented here for completeness. On success the engine redirects the user to the application's configured `redirectUri` with a one-shot authorization code attached.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: The application's public `clientId`.

parameters.name: scopes
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of scopes the application is requesting.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Opaque CSRF token echoed back on redirect for verification.

oauth.obtain-oauth-app-access-token.description: Server-to-server exchange. Authenticate with HTTP Basic using `base64(clientId:clientSecret)` — **not** the authorization code, which goes in the form body. See the [OAuth applications](/guides/oauth-applications.html) guide for the end-to-end flow.

parameters.name: grantType
parameters.description: Must be `authorization_code`.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The one-shot authorization code from the consent leg.

responses.description: Bad Basic-auth credentials, or code has expired / already been used.

oauth.refresh-oauth-app-access-token.description: Trade a valid refresh token for a new access + refresh token pair. The previous refresh token is rotated; the previous access token (if still valid) is invalidated immediately.

parameters.name: grantType
parameters.description: Must be `refresh_token`.

parameters.name: refreshToken
parameters.description: The refresh token from the most recent token-issue response.

responses.description: Fresh access + refresh token pair issued.
Bad Basic-auth credentials, or refresh token already used / revoked.

oauth.oauth-application-list.description: Returns every OAuth application registered under the authenticated profile, with scope and last-used metadata. Useful for account-management UIs that let users review and revoke integrations.

utility.check-server-health.description: Cheap liveness check. Returns 200 with a short status payload when the engine is up. Used as the hello-world call in [Getting started](/guides/getting-started.html).

utility.request-command-id-status.description: Inspect the current state of a command id without subscribing to the WebSocket. `command_status: 1` means still in flight, `2` means accepted/succeeded, `3` means rejected. Useful for one-shot scripts or for reconciling after a WebSocket reconnect.

parameters.name: commandId
parameters.description: The id returned by any async-command endpoint.

device.request-device-id.description: First step in the device-onboarding chain. See [Device management](/guides/device-management.html).

parameters.name: deviceName
parameters.description: Friendly display name for the device.

responses.description: Request accepted; await the WebSocket ack.

device.retrieve-device-id.description: Once the `X-DPT-CAB-ID` ack for [`request-device-id`](#tag/device/operation/device.request-device-id) has landed with `command_status: 2`, fetch the assigned `deviceId` with this call.

parameters.name: commandId
parameters.description: Command id from `request-device-id`.

device.approve-device.description: Owner consent step. After approval the device is active under the profile and can be specialised to a role (storage, authenticator).

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: The device id to approve.

responses.description: Approval accepted; await the WebSocket ack.

device.reject-device.description: Discard a pending device registration instead of approving it. Use when a device-request arrives that you don't recognise.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: The device id to reject.

device.download-device-configuration.description: Returns the AMQP credentials, vhost, and routing-key binding the device needs to connect to the broker. Treat the response as a secret — it's equivalent to an API key.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: The device to issue configuration for.

parameters.name: tags
parameters.description: Comma-separated list.

device.remove-device.description: Invalidates the device's credentials and deregisters any specialised role it held. See [Device management](/guides/device-management.html#removing-a-device).

storage.register-storage-device.description: After approval ([`approve-device`](#tag/device/operation/device.approve-device)), call this to register the device in the storage fan-out. See [Storage device management](/guides/storage-device-management.html).

storage.remove-storage.description: Stops fan-out to this device. Existing chunks on the device's disk are not reclaimed by the engine — handle decommissioning separately.

verified-authenticator.register-authenti.description: After approval, call this to make the device eligible to receive ballots. See [Voting-gated operations](/guides/voting-gated-operations.html).

verified-authenticator.voting-configurat.description: Defines a strategy and ballot deadline that policies can reference. Omit `deviceIdentifiers` to dispatch ballots to every registered authenticator.

parameters.name: strategy
parameters.description: Voting strategy — `unanimous` is canonical.

parameters.name: timeLimit
parameters.description: Per-ballot deadline, in seconds.

parameters.name: deviceIdentifiers
parameters.description: Optional comma-separated list of device ids to target. Omit to dispatch to all authenticators.

policy.register-policy.description: Links a voting configuration to one or more system events. `configuration` must be non-empty — provide at least one meaningful key. `applyingEventIds` comes from [`list-policy-templates`](#tag/policy/operation/policy.list-policy-templates).

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: JSON-serialised object of policy parameters. Cannot be empty.

parameters.name: votingConfigurationId
parameters.description: Id of the voting configuration to use.

parameters.name: applyingEventIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated event ids the policy gates.

policy.activate-policy.description: Once active, the gated events are held for a voting pool until it resolves.

policy.deactivate-policy.description: Stops gating future events without deleting the policy configuration.

policy.list-policy-templates.description: Each template carries an `events` dict mapping event names (e.g. `data_token.data_exchanged`) to numeric ids — look up the id here to pass to `applyingEventIds` when registering a policy.

voting.check-pool-status.description: Returns current ballot counts and pool state. Useful for dashboards, debugging, and observability tooling.

data-token.list-data-tokens.description: Paged list of tokens with their metadata. Use for management/audit tooling, not hot paths.

data-token.update-data-token.description: Change a token's `fileName`, `extension`, or `context` without touching the payload. Asynchronous; await the `X-DPT-CAB-ID` ack.

data-token.configure-information-securit.description: Sets a free-form metadata object for governance tooling. Schema is intentionally soft — the engine accepts arbitrary field names; pick one your organisation uses consistently.

data-token.delete-data.description: Removes the engine-side token record and propagates removal to every registered storage device. No per-device delete acknowledgement is surfaced; for auditable deletion, reconcile each device independently.

responses.description: Delete accepted; dispatched to storage devices.

profile.change-password.description: Rotate the profile's password. Asynchronous — await the `X-DPT-CAB-ID` ack. Does not invalidate existing authorization tokens; call [`revoke-authorization-token`](#tag/profile/operation/profile.revoke-authorization-token) separately if you need all sessions to re-authenticate.

parameters.name: newPassword
parameters.description: Entropy requirements apply.

profile.retrieve-profile-id.description: Returns the profile's ULID. Safe to share with third-party systems as a stable reference; distinct from the username and password.

profile.generate-qr-token-for-authorizat.description: Returns a short-lived token payload suitable for rendering as a QR code. Mobile authenticator apps scan it to pair with the profile without the three-step device-onboarding chain.

profile.retrieve-token-expiry.description: Useful for clients that schedule reauthorisation before a token lapses.

profile.reauthorize-authorization-token.description: Issues a refreshed token with a new ttl without requiring the profile's password. The old token is superseded; use the new token for subsequent requests.

parameters.name: ttl
parameters.description: New lifetime in seconds.

profile.revoke-authorization-token.description: Right call for logout and for suspected token leaks. After a successful revocation, the token returns 401 from that point on.
